task_completion.description: Verify the dashboard is named 'Baseline Deviation Analysis'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the text 'Baseline Deviation Analysis' in the dashboard title area or header of the Graphite web interface.

task_completion.description: Verify the first graph is titled 'CPU Deviation from Baseline' with the correct target.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a graph titled 'CPU Deviation from Baseline'. The target string in the legend or editor should include 'diffSeries' and 'movingAverage' applied to 'servers.ec2_instance_1.cpu.utilization'.

task_completion.description: Verify the second graph is titled 'Instance 1 CPU Fleet Share' with the correct target.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a graph titled 'Instance 1 CPU Fleet Share'. The target string in the legend or editor should include 'asPercent' and 'sumSeries' applied to the EC2 instance CPU metrics.

task_completion.description: Verify the third graph is titled 'Smoothed Traffic Rate'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a graph titled 'Smoothed Traffic Rate' on the dashboard.

task_completion.description: Verify the dashboard contains exactly three graphs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Count the number of graphs visible on the dashboard. There should be exactly three graphs present, matching the required titles.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Graphite web interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show the Graphite web interface being used in a browser, rather than terminal commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: Verify the graphs display actual time-series data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The graphs should display actual plotted lines (time-series data), not empty grids or error messages, indicating genuine metric querying.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not bypass the UI by writing a JSON file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should not be seen simply writing a JSON dashboard definition file in a text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected dashboard name is 'Baseline Deviation Analysis'. It must contain exactly three graphs with the titles: 'CPU Deviation from Baseline', 'Instance 1 CPU Fleet Share', and 'Smoothed Traffic Rate'. The result file is expected to be saved to /tmp/baseline_deviation_analysis_result.json.
